behavior_summary: When the book object is activated, prepare a long lore text and hand it to the engine's book-reading routine so the player sees the full book contents.
Before showing the text, set an info/metadata marker on the book object (small integer value pushed) — likely to mark that object information is present or updated.
Capture the return value from the Book::read call, apply an 'implies' style check, store the result into a temporary, then yield/suspend execution — the script yields after presenting the book.
notable_control_flow: Push object parameter (BP+06) and other context (including a pid-like value), then invoke a set-info operation to update the object's metadata.
Convert an in-script literal string to a pointer and call Book::read with the object pointer and the string pointer to present the book text.

Receive Book::read return value, run an 'implies' opcode on it, pop/store result into a temp slot, then suspend/return — indicates the script yields while the read UI is active or records the outcome.
open_questions: Exact semantics of opcode 77 ('set info'): which info key/index is being set and what visible gameplay effect that produces (inferred as marking object info).
Meaning and source of the two-byte 'indirect' data pushed to the set-info call — what object field or meta record does it reference?
Precise interpretation of the Book::read return value and the 'implies' opcode's usage in this context (what conditions cause different post-read behavior?).
Whether the pushed 'pid' value is always the player process id or a different interacting actor id in some contexts.

main_function.name: U8P_BRIBOOK3::0080
main_function.purpose: Display a long lore/book text to the player when the book object is used and mark the object with a small info flag so its metadata reflects the read/available information.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the book/book-object is activated by an actor (inferred from Book::read call and typical item-use handling).

function_map.name: U8P_BRIBOOK3::0080
function_map.role: Handle book activation: set object info and invoke Book::read to present a long text.

external_calls.purpose: Present/process the supplied book text string for the given book object so the player can read the long lore text.
Set a small info/metadata value on the book object (value pushed is 1) before presenting the text — likely to mark that the object has associated info or to attach a short info flag.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the book object is activated, prepare a long lore text and hand it to the engine's book-reading routine so the player sees the full book contents.
